original: Leo II ends his reign as Catholic Pope
St Paul I ends his reign as Catholic Pope
Fighters of the First Crusade defeat Kerbogha of Mosul
Battle of Sarmada - Emir Ilghazi defeats French Crusaders
1st Council of Lyon (13th ecumenical council) opens
Gent/Brugge/Ieper recognize Maximilian of Austria as regent of Netherlands
Spanish troops conquer Buren
Peace of Alès: Rights of French huguenots limited
French colony of Guadeloupe established in the Caribbean
Battle of Beresteczko between Poles and Ukrainians starts
Battle of Fehrbellin: Brandenburg-Prussian troops defeat Sweden
King Philip's War: Colonial militia destroys Wampanoag settlement at Mount Hope in Bristol, Rhode Island, in response to recent attacks at Swansea, Massachusetts
Riot after public execution in Amsterdam, 200+ killed
First reported counterfeiting attempt in Boston
Quakers open a school for blacks in Philadelphia
Charleston, South Carolina repulses British sea attack
Battle of Monmouth, New Jersey (General Washington beats Clinton)
Mary Ludwig Hayes "Molly Pitcher" aids American patriots
British troops land at Ensenada, Argentina
Colonel Robert Gibbon Johnson proves tomatoes are not poisonous by eating a tomato on the steps of a courthouse in Salem, New Jersey
Gerrit Moll measures the noise of guns
Three missionaries of the Paris Evangelical Missionary Society obtain permission from Chief Moshoeshoe (Moshesh) to found a mission station in Basutoland (now Lesotho)
The Théâtre de l'Académie Royale de Musique in Paris premieres the ballet Giselle
The saxophone is patented by Antoine-Joseph "Adolphe" Sax
Sigma Chi Fraternity is founded at Miami University
First dog show is held, in Newcastle upon Tyne, England
Leipzig Observatory discovers short-period (6.2 yrs) Comet d'Arrest
Day 4 of 7 Day Battle of Savage's Station [Garnett's Farm] in Virginia
American Civil War: Second Battle of Donaldsonville, Louisiana - Union forces successfully repel Confederate attack on Fort Butler, on the Mississippi River
The Army of the Potomac is disbanded
Amsterdam typographer strike
U.S. Congress creates federal holidays (New Year's Day, Independence Day, Thanksgiving Day, and Christmas Day), initially applicable only to federal employees
Freedmen's Bank closes
MLB Philadelphia Quakers (later Phillies) win most lopsided shut-out in franchise history, 24-0, over Hoosiers at Seventh Street Ballpark in Indianapolis, Indiana
Phillies tie club record of 16 straight victories
Labor Day established as a holiday for US federal employees
The Natal Legislature plans to introduce the Indian Franchise Bill, South Africa
El Salvador, Honduras and Nicaragua form Central American Union
Marquis C. de Bonchamps' expedition reaches Gore, Ethiopia
Germany, Italy, and the Austro-Hungarian Empire renew their Triple Alliance for six more years
US buys concession to build Panama canal from French for $40 million
US Congress authorizes Louisiana Purchase Expo $1 gold coin
International Anti-Military Cooperation (IAMV) forms in Amsterdam
SS Norge runs aground and sinks off Rockall in the North Atlantic; more than 635 die, the largest maritime loss of life until the Titanic
Joseph Caillaux forms government in France
Potato entrepreneurs begins in Amsterdam
First flight between Hawaiian islands
Boston Red Sox Carl Mays pitches a complete doubleheader against NY Yankees, winning 1st game, 2-0, losing 2nd game, 4-1 (Polo Grounds, NYC)
The Irish Civil War starts when Irish Free State forces attack anti-treaty republicans in Dublin
MLB Brooklyn Robins blow 7-0 lead, as Phillies win 8-7 at the Baker Bowl in Philadelphia
Test cricket umpire debut for Frank Chester, v South Africa at Lord's
Tornado strikes Sandusky and Lorain, Ohio, killing 93
Mercedes-Benz forms when the world's oldest automobile manufacturers DMG and Benz & Cie merge
Friedrich Schmiedl attempts rocket mail in Austria (unsuccessful)
NY Governor Alfred E. Smith is nominated for US President at the Democratic Convention
First night game in Detroit at the newly built Hamtramck Stadium as the Negro League Detroit Stars take on the KC Monarchs
Adolf Hitlerflies to Essen, Germany for the "Night of the Long Knives"
Earl Averill's consecutive-game streak ends at 673
Japanese puppet state of Mengjiang is formed in northern China
Spanish pianist José Iturbi and his sister Amparo have their US debut performance as a piano duo, with the Philadelphia Orchestra at the Robin Hood Dell in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Pan Am opens southern route transatlantic air service (Dixie Clipper)
Yanks hit 13 HRs, sweep A's 23-2 & 10-0
"Quiz Kids?" premieres on radio
Romania cedes Bessarabia to Soviet Union
German and Romanian soldiers kill 11,000 Jews in Kishinev
German troops occupy Galicia, Poland
Col-gen Von Hoth' 6th Pantser enters Voronezj
Polish Provisional government of National Unity set up by Soviets
Enrico de Nicola becomes 1st President of Italy
Permanent radio play-by-play of Cleveland Indians games begins
"Tim-Tayshun" ("Temptation") novelty single by Red Ingle with Jo Stafford (billed as Cinderella G. Stump) hits #1
British begin airlift “Operation Plainfare” to West Berlin
North Korean forces capture Seoul, South Korea in opening phase of the Korean War
American radio sitcom about black characters "Amos 'n' Andy" premieres on CBS TV
111°F (44°C) at Camden, South Carolina (state record)
Riots break out in Poznan, Poland, 38 die
The first atomic reactor built for private research operates in Chicago, Illinois
Reds' fans stuff ballot box, electing 8 Reds as All Star starters
American swimmer Nancy Ramey sets world record for 100m butterfly in 1:09.6 in Los Angeles, California
Meldrim trestle disaster; freight train derails over Ogeechee River in Georgia causing IPG tanks to explode killing 23
Philadelphia Phillies' Wally Post is only outfielder to throw out 2 runners in an inning twice, in a 6-0 loss to Giants at Seals Stadium, San Francisco, California
10.40" (26.42 cm) of rainfall, Dunmor, Kentucky (state 24-hour record)
Phils and San Francisco set then record longest night game (5h11m) 7-7 15 innings tie
Thalidomide drug banned in Netherlands
Belaunde Terry inaugurated as President of Peru
Dutch Princess Beatrice and Claus von Amsberg announce their engagement
Ernie Terrellbeats Doug Jones by unanimous points decision over 15 rounds in Houston to retain his WBA heavyweight boxing title
George Harrisonis fined £6 for speeding
Israel annexes East Jerusalem
Daniel Ellsberg indicted for leaking the "Pentagon Papers" to the New York Times
John Hampshire scores 107 on Test cricket debut v WI at Lord's
Police carry out an early morning raid on gay bar Stonewall Inn, Greenwich Village, NY; about 400 to 1,000 patrons riot against police, it lasts 3 days. Beginning of the modern LGBT rights movement
Around 500 Catholic workers at the Harland and Wolff shipyard are forced to leave their work by Protestant employees as serious rioting continues in Belfast
MLB Pittsburgh Pirates sweep a doubleheader, 3-2 and 4-1, against the Chicago Cubs in final two games at Forbes Field, the Pirates home since 1909, in front of 40,918 fans
Lawsuit in Detroit challenges Little League's "no girls" rule
New Zealand ship HMNZS Otago sails for Mururoa nuclear test zone after France’s refusal to accept an International Court of Justice injunction against its atmospheric nuclear testing
Northern Ireland Assembly elections take place
Fall of earth and rocks kill 200 (Quebrada Blanca Canyon, Colombia)
The South African rugby team beats France 33-18 in Pretoria
First woman is admitted to the Air Force Academy in Colorado Springs, Colorado
Three British mercenaries are sentenced to death for their part in the Angolan civil war
Billy Hunter becomes Rangers' 4th manager in 6 days
Supreme Court allows Federal control of Nixon tapes papers
The United Party, main political opposition party in South Africa, is formally disbanded by the majority faction after members leave the party to join other new political parties
Supreme Court orders Cal medical school to admit Allan Bakke a white man claiming reverse discrimination when application was rejected
UNICEF chooses rock group Kansas as Ambassadors of Goodwill
OPEC raises oil prices 24%
NYC transit fare rises from 50 cents to 60 cents
The South African Springbok rugby team lead by Morné du Plessis beats the British Lions 12-10 in Port Elizabeth to lead 3-0 in the series
74 government officials die in attack on Islamic Republican Party conference in Tehran, Iran, including Chief Justice Mohammad Beheshti
Bridge section along I-95 in Greenwich, Connecticut collapses, killing 3
NASA launches Galaxy-A
Former member of South African Congress of Trade Unions (SACTU), Jeannette Schoon, and her six-year-old daughter, Katryn, are killed by a letter bomb at Lubango, in northern Angola
Discovery ferried back to Kennedy Space Center via Bergstrom AFB, Tx
Irish population condemns divorce
Kenneth and Nellie Pike challenge Ala Dem runoff win by AG C Graddick
West European leaders, meeting in the Netherlands, delay indefinitely imposing economic sanctions against South Africa
Boston Red Sox outfielder Don Baylor sets MLB career hit-by-pitch mark at 244 when plunked by Rick Rhoden in 6-2 win overthe New York Yankees
South Africa signs the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty
Burharnuddin Rabbani becomes president of Afghanistan
Italian government of Amato forms
LA Police commissioner Daryl Gates steps down
The Constitution of Estonia is signed into law
Two earthquakes, including the third strongest in the US (7.4), rock California
US Dream Team beats Cuba in 1st exhibition basketball game, 133-57
Carlton Fisk, 45, released by White Sox, as all time leader of most games caught and most HRs by a catcher
Doctors recommend ligament transplant to JoseCanseco's arm
Jacques Lemaire is named NJ Devils 8th Head Coach
NCRV shows last "Cheers" in Netherlands
MLB New York Mets pitcherDwight Goodensuspended for 60 days due to drug charges
NHL Draft: Windsor Spitfires (OHL) defenceman Ed Jovanovski first pick by Florida Panthers
NBA draft: Maryland power forward Joe Smith first pick by Golden State Warriors
NJ Devils Stanley Cup Victory Party, admidst rumours they were moving to Nashville, goalie Chris Terreri holds up "Nashville? NO WAY!" sign
The Constitution of Ukraine is signed into law.
American TV evangelist Robert Schuller (70) attacks a male flight attendant (33), after disputes regarding during a luggage stowage, and cheese
Cuban exile Elián González returns to Cuba following a Supreme Court order.
NBA Draft: Cincinnati power forward Kenyon Martin first pick by New Jersey Nets
In South Africa, the Congress of South African Trade Unions and the Treatment Action Campaign table a national HIV/AIDS treatment plan in the National Economic, Development and Labour Council
Estonia, Lithuania and Slovenia join the European Exchange Rate Mechanism
Sovereign power is handed to the interim government of Iraq by the Coalition Provisional Authority, ending the U.S.-led rule of that nation
A final design for Manhattan's Freedom Tower is formally unveiled
NBA Draft: Utah center Andrew Bogut first pick by Milwaukee Bucks

Operation Red Wings: 3 members of US Navy SEAL reconnaissance team killed on Sawtalo Sar Mountain, Kunar, Afghanistan, in an ambush; 2 helicopters dispatched to assist shot down, killing an additional 16 US troops
NBA Draft: Italian power forward Andrea Bargnani is first pick by Toronto Raptors
The Republic of Montenegro admitted as the 192nd Member of the United Nations by General Assembly resolution 60/264
Craig Biggio of the Houston Astros becomes the 27th member of the 3000 hit club, going 5 for 5 against the Colorado Rockies
NBA Draft: Ohio State center Greg Oden first pick by Portland Trail Blazers
Play "August: Osage County", written by Tracy Letts, premieres at the Steppenwolf Theatre in Chicago (Pulitzer Prize for Drama 2008)
A series of car bombs in Iraq kills 14 and injures 50 people
Jay Onraitand Dan O'Toole's final "Sportscentre" broadcast on TSN
David Sweat shot and captured near Canadian border. 2nd prisoner to escape maximum-security Clinton Correctional Facility June 6
Greek Credit Crisis: Greek government says banks closed for a week and ATM withdrawals restricted after European Central Bank refused to supply emergency funds
Suicide bombings and gun attacks at Istanbul's Ataturk Airport kill 42 and wound more than 200
Wilshire Grand Center becomes the tallest building in Los Angeles and in the US west of the Mississippi at 1,100 ft
Amsterdam elects its first-ever woman mayor Femke Halsema since its first mayor in 1343
Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo declared "largely contained" by World Health Organization, first outbreak to use new Merck vaccine
Employee charged with attempted poisoning of colleague's sandwich in Schloss Holte-Stukenbrock, Germany, leading authorities to investigate 21 other suspicious deaths
Fire starts on Winter Hill on Lancashire moorland, England, lit by arson
Lone gunman attacks offices of Capital Gazette newspaper in Annapolis, Maryland, killing nine
Power company uncovers Neolithic wooden trackway 2,300 years old in Suffolk, England. One of the largest archaeological digs in Europe at 16,000 square meters
3,400 year old Bronze Age palace from the Mittani Empire uncovered on the banks of the Tigris River, due to lack of rainfall dropping the water level in the Mosul Dam reservoir
EU and South American trade bloc Mercosur agree on a historic trade deal, the EU's biggest to date
Highest-ever temperature recorded in France 45.9°C in Gallargues-le-Montueux, southern France
50th anniversary of 1st Gay Pride march in New York City marked around the world
COVID-19 cases surge in southern and western US states with California, Texas and Florida closing bars, Arizona reports 20% of tests returning as positive
Global death toll from COVID-19 passes 500,000, doubling in less than two months (Johns Hopkins)
AirCar, prototype flying car capable of flying 1,000km (600 miles), at height of 8,200ft (2,500m), completes 35 min test flight between Nitra and Bratislava airports in Slovakia
Mexico's Supreme Court decriminalizes marijuana use by adults
Tigray Defense Forces retake Tigray's regional capital of Mekelle in Ethiopia's Tigray War. The Ethiopian government declares a unilateral ceasefire to save face but neither side sticks to it.
US Supreme Court declines to hear school's appeal in transgender bathroom case made by Gavin Grimm in Virginia, upholding an earlier decision it was discriminatory
Ghislaine Maxwell sentenced to 20 years in US federal prison for grooming and aiding Jeffery Epstein to abuse underage girls
In dramatic testimony to the Jan 6 Committee hearing, a senior White House aide testifies Trump wanted to march to the Capitol and his fury when he couldn't
Rocket Lab launches NASA's CAPSTONE, a microwave-oven sized CubeSat spacecraft from Mahia peninsula, New Zealand, to orbit the Moon, research for future Gateway and Artemis missions
New York Yankees' pitcher Domingo Germán (30) throws a perfect game in 11-0 win over Oakland A’s at Oakland-Alameda County Coliseum; it is his first complete game in 7-year MLB career
South Koreans become a year or two younger as country drops two traditional age-counting systems to align with international standards (used to be aged one at birth)
Boy band Seventeen are the first K-pop group to play the main Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury
US Supreme Court decision clears the way for states to place bans on homeless sleeping, as 650,000 now estimated homeless in the US, up 12%
An estimated 140,000 people protest in Belgrade and other Serbian cities, opposing the Serbian government and calling for snap elections and the removal of President Aleksandar Vučić
More than 100,000 people march through Budapest, Hungary despite a government ban of the annual Pride parade; prior to the ban the event held since 1995 usually involved only a few thousand people
United Kingdom Border Force seizes 2.4 metric tons of cocaine worth US$132 million, one of the country's largest drug busts
YorkistEdward IVcrowned King of England after deposingHenry VIfrom the House of Lancaster
King Carlos I is elected Holy Roman EmperorCharles V
Charles III, Duke of Bourbonoccupies Provence during his dispute with French KingFrancis I
French aristocrat and new Governor of New FranceLouis de Buade de Frontenacsets sail from La Rochelle for Quebec
Siege of Cuneo ends in a French defeat and a loss of 700–800 troops after Imperial cavalry arrives to relieve forces led by Duke of SavoyVictor Amadeus II(Nine Years' War)
Russian TsarinaCatherine IIseizes power, declaring herself sovereign ruler of Russia
Australian bushrangerNed Kellycaptured at Glenrowan
PainterPaul Gauguinleaves France for Tahiti for the second time and never returns
MLB Washington Senators steal a record 13 bases off of New York Highlanders catcherBranch Rickey
First airship with passengers, theZeppelin LZ7Deutschland, makes its maiden voyage and gets stuck in trees on Mount Limberg, Lower Saxony, injuring one crew member
Queen Wilhelminaand Prince Henry of the Netherlands state visit to London, England
Louis Armstrongmakes a 78 rpm recording of "West End Blues"
FDRorders a federal gold vault to be built at Fort Knox, Kentucky
Joe Louisbeats Tony Galento by TKO at 2:29 in round 4 at Yankee Stadium, NYC in his 7th heavyweight boxing title defense
MLB CommissionerFord Frickoverrules fan voting, due to ballot stuffing in Cincinnati, and namesStan Musial,Willie Mays, &Hank Aaronto the All-Star team
US Open Women's Golf, Forest Lake CC:Mickey Wrightwins her first of four Open titles by five strokes overLouise Suggs; she is the first player to win both the Open and LPGA in the same year
Soviet leaderNikita Khrushchevvisits East Berlin
Organization of Afro-American Unity formed in New York byMalcolm X
1st US ground combat forces in Vietnam authorized by PresidentLyndon B. Johnson
US Supreme Court (8-0) overturns draft evasion conviction ofMuhammad Ali
Black Sports Hall of Fame forms:Paul Robeson,Elgin Baylor,Jesse Owens,Jim Brown,Wilma Rudolph,Joe LouisandAlthea Gibsonelected
Paul McCartney& Wings release singles "Band on the Run" and "Zoo Gang" in UK
Prince CharlesandDiana, Princess of Wales,name their baby "William"
Mike Tysonsues to break contract with manager Bill Cayton
Jonah Lomubecomes the youngest-ever All Black at 19 yrs 45 days playing rugby for New Zealand against France in Christchurch
Remake of "The Nutty Professor" starringEddie Murphyopens in theaters in the USA
ProfessorStephen Hawkinghosts a 'party for time travelers at the University of Cambridge, not sending out the invites until after the party
Christine Lagardebecomes the 1st women to be elected head of the International Monetary Fund (IMF)
NBA Draft: Kentucky centerAnthony Davisfirst pick New Orleans Pelicans
"Spider-man: Homecoming" directed by Jon Watts starringTom Holland,Michael KeatonandRobert Downey Jr. premieres in Los Angeles
China’s president,Xi Jinpingbegins 3 day trip to Hong Kong to mark 20 years since the territory handed back to China
Final draft of Declaration of Independence submitted to Continental Congress
Coronation ofQueen Victoria, aged 19, during a five-hour ceremony at Westminster Abbey, London
Franz Ferdinand, Archduke of Austria and his wife Sophie are assassinated by Bosnian-Serb assassin Gavrilo Princip in Sarajevo, setting off a chain of alliances and events that lead to World War I
Treaty of Versailles is signed in France, ending World War I and establishing the League of Nations
Go-Fukakusa, 89th Emperor of Japan (1246-60), born in Heian Kyō, Japan (d. 1304)
